title: Gaor Tembon

{{Quote|Gaor Tembon represents an interesting case. Though it is without question that he has committed atrocities, his recent actions have been of great help to us. However, I cannot condone the activities of a form slaver — despite his seeming "change of face," he must be brought to justice!|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]]|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Gaor Tembon''' was a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] who operated in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] during the first years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. His base of operations was in the [[Nexer system/Legends|Nexer system]] and he traveled in [[Nestt-class light freighter|''Nestt''-class freighter]] called the ''[[Miv'rah Firestorm]]''.

==Biography==
Gaor Tembon was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] in [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]]. A renowned [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]], Tembon was never captured by any authorities, although several operatives of his were. The Sullustan had a reputation for never refusing a customer or cargo, including the transportation of [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. Tembon was popular with several organizations, including the [[Riders of the Maelstrom]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and criminal organizations including [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Kajidic/Legends|kajidics]].<ref name="WBC" />

The operatives captured by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] indicated that Tembon had a change of heart, and was willing to cut of all ties to the Empire and to no longer transport slave cargo. The change in attitude was reportedly due to the Empire's attempts to forcibly acquire some of his [[Starship/Legends|starships]], a move that infuriated Tembon. While the New Republic considered the information suspect, the information from Tembon's operatives indicated that he was operating out of the [[Nexer system/Legends|Nexer system]]. The New Republic posted a reward for Tembon's capture; 30,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on the charges of smuggling, transportation of slaves, conspiracy to deprive sapients of their freedom, and manslaughter.<ref name="WBC" />

==Personality and traits==
Gaor Tembon was a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who stood 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. He was an effective con man, shrewd bargainer, and [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Skills and abilities==
Tembon was skilled in the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]], and stealth techniques. He had knowledge of [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] procedures, languages, and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]], and a number of underworld contacts. Tembon was a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], adept at flying [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and smaller vessels, [[Astrogation/Legends|navigating]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and operating starship weapons and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. He was also adept at repairing starfighters and bypassing security systems.<ref name="WBC" />

==Equipment==
Tembon was armed with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], and owned a highly modified [[Nestt-class light freighter|''Nestt''-class light freighter]] named the ''[[Miv'rah Firestorm]]''. It had concealed [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]].<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gaor Tembon was profiled in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], published by [[West End Games]] in [[1993]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. [[Mike Jackson]] provided Tembon's illustration.
